questions.question: Which cellular organelle is responsible for generating most of the cell's adenosine triphosphate (ATP)?
What biological molecules act as catalysts to accelerate chemical reactions without being consumed in the process?
During which phase of mitosis do the sister chromatids separate and move toward opposite poles of the cell?
What is the primary structural lipid component that forms the selectively permeable bilayer of cell membranes?
Which organelle is studded with ribosomes and is primarily involved in protein synthesis and folding?
What is the name of the metabolic pathway that breaks down glucose into pyruvate in the cytoplasm, generating a net profit of 2 ATP?
Which cellular process involves the engulfing of large solid particles or whole cells by wrapping the plasma membrane around them?
What is the name of the programming mechanism that triggers scheduled, deliberate cell suicide (programmed cell death)?
Which organelle contains hydrolytic enzymes used to break down waste materials, cellular debris, and foreign invaders?
What molecule acts as the final electron acceptor at the very end of the aerobic electron transport chain?
